Ground Shock.UQ
Project 3  —  Probabilistic Ground Shock

PPV is a
Distribution,
not a point value.

Gaussian process regression with range-dependent noise, trained on 800 synthetic UFC ground-shock samples. Returns a calibrated probability band at any scaled distance — then checks whether the bands actually hold on held-out data.

Matérn 5/2 Kernel Heteroscedastic Noise Calibrated Intervals Adam Inversion
—
90% band coverage
800/100
train / test split
5
optimizer restarts
Z ∈ [1, 100]
ft / lb⅓ range
Week 1 Deliverable

Forward Prediction with Uncertainty

Pick a scaled distance. The GP returns a mean PPV plus 50/80/90/95% predictive bands, all learned from synthetic data with range-dependent scatter — closer shots noisier than far ones.

—
90% obs. coverage
Query Point
Scaled Distance Z
25.0ft / lb⅓
1.0100.0
Query is outside the training range. The GP is extrapolating — treat the band width as approximate.
Slider covers the training range (~1–100 ft/lb⅓). You can type a value outside that range; the prediction still works but the uncertainty band is wider and less reliable far from the data.
UFC Reference Equations
UFC Eq. 2-85 — true curve the GP learns from
VV = 150 / Z1.5  (in/sec)
GP kernel — Matérn 5/2 + linear mean
k(x,x') = σf²(1+√5r+5r²/3)e−√5r
Heteroscedastic noise — per-point diagonal
Σii = (noise_scale × σi)²
Predictive Intervals at Queried Z
50%
—
80%
—
90%
—
95%
—
GP Fit: Mean + Confidence Bands
90% band   GP mean   UFC true   test points
Calibration Check
Do the 90% bands actually cover 90% of test points?
Each dot compares a nominal confidence level to the fraction of held-out test points that actually landed inside the band. Points on the diagonal mean the intervals are trustworthy. The green strip is a ±5% sampling allowance for a 100-point test set.
Week 2 Deliverable

Inverse Soil-Parameter Recovery

Given a handful of field measurements (Z, V pairs), recover the site-specific attenuation exponent n and coupling constant k by gradient descent in log space — the reverse of the forward problem.

Field Measurements
Z (ft/lb⅓) V (in/sec)
Simulate generates 7 noisy readings from a soil with n = 2.80, k = 175.0 — different from the training model — so you can see whether recovery actually works. Editing the table manually runs the same optimizer without any ground truth to compare against.
Optimizer
Objective — log-space weighted least squares
min Σ (ln Vobs − ln k + n ln Z)²
Adam update — 300 epochs, lr = 0.01
params ← params − lr · m̂ / (√v̂ + ε)
Recovered vs. True Parameters

Recovered

Attenuation exponent n—
Coupling constant k—

True (simulated soil)

Attenuation exponent n—
Coupling constant k—
Fit vs. Field Readings
field readings   recovered curve   true curve
Optimizer Convergence
Adam loss (MSE in log space) over 300 epochs
